name: Fry Bread, My Personal Recipe (Baking Powder Recipe)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup warm milk (water can be substituted if desired)
recipeInstructions:
Fry bread is one of the most popular and widespread modern Native American Indian foods. There are two main types: a fried yeast bread common in the North and East, and a baking powder-based version used more in the South and West. In Texas, the Alabama-Coushatta use the same recipe as the Navajo Fry Bread listed below.
1. In a mixing bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. Stir in milk and knead briefly with lightly oiled hands until smooth.
3. Rub the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil over the dough.
4. Cover and let sit in a warm area for about 30 minutes.
5. Shape enough dough into a ball that fits in the palm of your hand, forming it into a circle about one-quarter inch thick.
6. Deep fry in hot oil (350°F) for about one minute per side or until golden brown.
7. Makes 10-12 pieces.
Note: The dough can also be shaped into triangles, squares, or perfect circles using a cookie cutter if rolled out. Serve with honey, maple syrup, or as bread for meals.
